_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-08 15:25:55Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I found the issue!

Page background colors:
- Light mode: `--color-root-bg: rgb(250, 247, 240)` — a warm off-white
- Dark mode: `--color-root-bg: rgb(28, 28, 30)` — a dark gray

But `neutral-1` is:
- Light mode: `#ffffff` — pure white
- Dark mode: `#09090b` — near black

So the edge effect gradient `from-neutral-1` uses:
- Light: `#ffffff` (pure white) against a `rgb(250, 247, 240)` (warm off-white) background — slightly visible mismatch


- Dark: `#09090b` (near black) against `rgb(28, 28, 30)` (dark gray) — a stark contrast that creates that visible dark strip the user is seeing

The solution is to change the gradient to use the actual page background color instead, so the edges blend seamlessly.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-08 15:25:57Z)**_

问题明矣。

页面实际背景为 `--color-root-bg`：
- Light: `rgb(250, 247, 240)` (暖白)
- Dark: `rgb(28, 28, 30)` (`#1c1c1e`)

而 `neutral-1` 为：
- Light: `#ffffff` (纯白)
- Dark: `#09090b` (近黑)

暗色模式下 `#09090b` 远深于 `#1c1c1e`，遂生"黑边"。

Using CSS `mask-image` on the skewed background div itself would be a better approach - it would make the edges fade out naturally regardless of what's behind it.

The idea is to apply a mask to the skewed background div so its right edge fades out, instead of overlaying a colored gradient on top.

1. Remove the edge effect overlay div entirely
2. Apply a `mask-image` gradient to the skewed background div (or the outer container) so the right edge fades out naturally

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-08 15:38:12Z)**_

改毕。以 `mask-image` 替代覆盖层：

- 移除旧之 `from-neutral-1` 渐变覆盖 div
- 于外层容器加 `[mask-image:linear-gradient(to_right,black_calc(100%-2rem),transparent)]`

如此，右侧 2rem 自然淡出，无论背景为何色、何纹理，皆可无缝融合。
